back_translation: Now on a Sabbath day, when He went into the house of one of the leading men among the Pharisees to eat a meal, they were watching Him closely.
Behold, there was a certain man who had the disease of dropsy before Him.
Jesus responded to the situation and asked the experts in the law and the Pharisees, «Is it lawful according to the law to heal on the Sabbath or not?»
But they remained silent. So He took hold of the man, healed him, and let him go.
He said to them, «Among you, if anyone has a son or an ox that falls into a well on the Sabbath day, will he not immediately pull it out at once?»
They were unable to answer these things.
When He noticed how the invited guests chose the place of honor, He told them a parable, saying,
«When someone invites you to a wedding banquet, do not recline in the highest place of honor, in case he has invited someone more honorable than you.
Then the host who invited both you and him together will come and say to you, ‹Yield this seat to this man,› and then you will have to retreat to sit in the lowest place in shame.

But when someone invites you, go and recline in the lowest place, so that when the host who invited you comes, he may say to you, ‹Friend, please move up higher,› and then you will receive honor before all who are reclining with you.
For everyone who exalts himself will be made low, and he who humbles himself will be lifted up.»

Then He also said to the one who had invited Him, «When you hold a midday meal or evening banquet, do not invite your friends or your brothers or your relatives or rich neighbors, in case they invite you in return, and it becomes a repayment to you.
But when you hold a banquet, invite the poor, the crippled, the lame, and the blind.
Then you will be blessed, because they cannot repay you, and God will repay you on the day when the righteous rise from the dead.»
When one of those reclining with Him heard these things, he said to Him, «Whoever gets to eat food in the kingdom of God — that one is blessed.»
He said to him, «A certain man prepared a great evening banquet and invited many people,
and when the time for the banquet came, he sent his servant to tell those who had been invited, ‹Come, for everything is ready now.›
But they all together with one accord began to excuse themselves. The first said to the servant, ‹I have just bought a field, and I must go out to see it; please excuse me from coming.›
Another said, ‹I have bought five pairs of oxen, and I am going to test them; please excuse me from coming.›
Another said, ‹I have just married, so I cannot come.›

The servant returned and reported these things to his master. Then the owner of the house became angry, and he commanded the servant, ‹Go out quickly into the streets and alleys of the city, and bring in here the poor, the crippled, the blind, and the lame.›
The servant came back and reported, ‹Master, what you commanded has been done, but there is still room.›
The master commanded the servant, ‹Go out along the highways and the paths by the field-hedges, and urge people strongly to come in, so that our house may be full.
I tell you all that not one of the men originally invited will taste the food at our banquet.›»
Large crowds were traveling along with Him. He turned and said to them,
«If anyone comes to Me and does not hate his father and mother, his wife, his children, his brothers and sisters — even his own life as well — that one cannot be My disciple.
Whoever does not bear his own cross and follow Me cannot be My disciple.
For among you, is there anyone who wishes to build a tower but does not first sit down and calculate the expense — whether he has enough to complete it?
Otherwise, when he has laid the foundation but cannot finish it, everyone who sees will begin to mock him,
saying, ‹This man began to build but could not finish it.›

Or what king who will go to make war against another king will not first sit down to consider whether he is able to use ten thousand troops to confront the one coming to fight him with twenty thousand?
If he is unable, he will send a delegation while the other party is still far away, to ask for terms of peace.
In the same way, everyone among you who does not give up all that he has — that one cannot be My disciple.
Salt is a good thing, but if the salt loses its saltiness entirely, with what can anyone season it to bring it back?
It cannot be used for the soil, nor can it be used for the compost pile; so they throw it outside. Whoever has ears to hear, let him hear.»
